licenses.name: Eclipse Public License 2.0
licenses.description: File-level weak copyleft; modifications to EPL files must be shared. Allows combining with GPL-2.0+ under certain conditions.

compatibility.notes: Can be combined with GPL-2.0+ via secondary license provision. File-level copyleft similar to MPL. Includes explicit patent grant and patent termination clause.

metadata.explanation_hint: Used by Eclipse IDE, Jakarta EE, and many Java projects. Includes an explicit patent grant and a patent termination clause. Copyleft is per 'module', not per file.

licenses.name: zlib License
licenses.description: Very permissive; modified versions must be marked as changed and cannot be misrepresented as original. Common in game development.

compatibility.notes: Highly permissive. Modified versions must be marked as changed but no copyleft or attribution required in binaries. Compatible with most licenses.

metadata.explanation_hint: Used by zlib, libpng, SDL. Unlike MIT, you must mark modified sources as changed. No attribution required in binaries — common in game engines and embedded systems.

licenses.name: PostgreSQL License
licenses.description: Short permissive license similar to MIT/BSD, used exclusively by the PostgreSQL project.

compatibility.notes: Functionally identical to MIT. Compatible with virtually all licenses. Used exclusively by PostgreSQL — no practical reason to use it for new projects.

metadata.explanation_hint: Essentially MIT-equivalent. Used only by PostgreSQL. No practical reason to use it for new projects — choose MIT or BSD-2 instead.
